Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), and urine analysis shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible renal pathology or urinary tract issues. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, raising concern for hypoglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation of renal function, glycemic status, and possible underlying causes for abnormal urine findings.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
